Dashboard archiveTechnical Analysis hub

Combined market dashboard

The U.S. index trend is broadly constructive: all 15 index charts are above their 50- and 200-day averages, 13 of 15 are above the 20-day EMA, and every RSI is above 50. Sector breadth is positive but less complete—9 of 11 sectors are above the 200-day average, 7 are above the 50-day average and 6 are above the 20-day EMA—so the tape is healthy but leadership is not fully broad.

15 / 15Indexes above 50-day and 200-day
13 / 15Indexes above 20-day EMA
9 / 11Sectors above 200-day average
6 / 11Sectors above 20-day EMA
Primary trend: constructiveLeadership: XLK + XLEParticipation: moderateRotation: mixed cyclical / defensive

Technology and Energy remain the clearest relative leaders. Financials are breaking out and improving quickly; Industrials retain a strong absolute trend but have recently softened versus SPY. Health Care, Utilities and Real Estate are constructive in absolute terms yet still lag the benchmark, while Staples remains weak. Communication Services and Consumer Discretionary are the main structural laggards. The combination suggests a pro-risk market with improving cyclical pockets, but not a clean, broad rotation across all growth-sensitive sectors.

Tab 1 · US Indexes

Broad trend and participation

Source: US Indexes ChartList

15charts · 2026-07-14

Executive summary

Primary trend breadth is excellent. Every index, cap band and broad-market ETF sits above rising 50- and 200-day averages, and only Mid Caps and IWM are fractionally below their 20-day EMAs. Momentum is uniformly above the RSI 50 line.

Cross-chart themes

Leadership is no longer confined to the largest technology names: transports, the Value Line Arithmetic Index, small caps and micro caps all retain constructive trends. Nasdaq Composite and Nasdaq 100 are consolidating rather than breaking down, while Utilities also participate, showing that breadth includes defensive areas.

US Indexes bottom line

The trend remains bullish and broadly confirmed. The next confirmation would be simultaneous new highs in SPX/IWV, transports and the small/micro-cap complex. The main warning would be a cluster of closes below 50-day averages—especially Nasdaq 100 below 29,474, SPX below 7,447 and IWM below 288.98.

Chart data date: 2026-07-14. ChartList count: 15. Open source page.

Tab 2 · US Sectors - Cap Weight

Leadership, participation and rotation

Source: US Sectors - Cap Weight ChartList

12charts · 2026-07-14

Executive summary

SPY remains in an uptrend. Of 11 sectors, 9 are above the 200-day average, 7 are above the 50-day average and 6 are above the 20-day EMA. Technology and Energy are the clear relative leaders; Financials are the strongest fresh breakout. The participation profile is positive but not yet uniformly broad.

Rotation and breadth

Cyclical rotation is mixed: Energy and Financials strengthen, Industrials remain constructive, but Consumer Discretionary is a major laggard and Materials is range-bound. Defensive participation is also mixed: Health Care, Utilities and Real Estate are above all major averages, while Staples is weak. Breadth is therefore stable-to-moderate rather than decisively broadening.

US Sectors bottom line

Leadership remains concentrated in Technology and Energy, with Financials providing the most important broadening signal. A durable XLF breakout, an XLI relative-strength turn higher and XLY reclaiming its 50-/200-day cluster would confirm wider cyclical participation. Failure by XLK to hold 181.81 or XLE to hold 55.11, combined with renewed weakness in XLY/XLC, would indicate narrowing leadership.

Chart data date: 2026-07-14. ChartList count: 12 (SPY benchmark plus 11 sectors). Open source page.
